theme: A comprehensive benchmarking of AI inference hardware and software frameworks using InferenceXv2, emphasizing the critical role of advanced techniques like Disaggregated Prefill and Wide Expert Parallelism in optimizing the interactivity-throughput tradeoff and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) across NVIDIA and AMD ecosystems.
key_arguments: InferenceXv2 provides continuous, dynamic benchmarking to track rapid, weekly software improvements across major GPU SKUs, moving beyond static point-in-time metrics.
The core economics of LLM serving rely on balancing interactivity (latency per user) and throughput (total tokens), a tradeoff akin to choosing between a race car and a metro bus.

Advanced optimization techniques, specifically Disaggregated Prefill combined with Wide Expert Parallelism (WideEP), are essential for achieving Pareto optimal performance in modern MoE models like DeepSeek R1.

While AMD's MI355X hardware shows competitive raw throughput in FP8, NVIDIA maintains dominance through a mature software ecosystem (TRTLLM) that handles complex, composed optimizations (like FP4 + Disagg + WideEP) which AMD currently struggles to support simultaneously.
Isolated software optimizations, such as AMD's ATOM engine, fail in real-world production if they lack composability and support for essential features like KV cache offloading and tool parsing.
structure_summary: The article opens with an introduction to the InferenceXv2 benchmark and a primer on foundational technical concepts (tradeoffs, TP/EP/DP). It then dives into empirical benchmark results for DeepSeek R1 and GPT-OSS 120B, analyzing the impact of Disaggregated Serving and WideEP. Following this, it contrasts NVIDIA and AMD's hardware (Hopper/Blackwell vs. MI300/355) and software ecosystems (TRTLLM vs. SGLang/ATOM). The piece concludes with discussions on unit economics, accuracy evaluations, project infrastructure updates (Claude AI usage, GitHub Actions), and the future roadmap.

terminology.context_meaning: The process of performing prefill and decode phases of LLM inference on separate nodes to optimize compute and memory utilization.
terminology.translation: Prefill-Decode 分离 / 首代词分离计算
terminology.rationale: Crucial architectural concept in modern inference. Needs explicit translation and English retention on first use for clarity.

terminology.context_meaning: Distributing expert weights of an MoE model across a large number of GPUs to handle massive active parameters.
terminology.rationale: A highly specific optimization technique highlighted in the article. Keep English acronym for technical accuracy.

terminology.context_meaning: Measured in tok/s/user, describing how fast each user receives tokens (latency-focused).
terminology.rationale: In standard Chinese AI literature, this might just be called '延迟' (latency), but the authors specifically contrast 'interactivity' with 'throughput'. Needs to be translated literally but annotated.

terminology.context_meaning: Measured in tok/s, describing the total tokens a system generates across all users (capacity-focused).
A collective communication operation where all processes reduce data and receive the result.
terminology.rationale: Standard distributed training/inference term. Best kept in English with '集合通信' appended for clarity.

terminology.context_meaning: A communication operation where every process sends data to every other process, heavily used in EP.
terminology.rationale: Crucial for understanding the bottleneck of Expert Parallelism.

terminology.context_meaning: The ability of different software optimizations (FP4, WideEP, Disagg) to be enabled simultaneously without breaking.
terminology.rationale: In software engineering, this refers to features working together. Mistranslating as hardware modularity would ruin the argument.

terminology.context_meaning: A speculative decoding technique to draft multiple tokens ahead.
terminology.rationale: Core optimization technique discussed in the DeepSeek context.

terminology.context_meaning: Moving the Key-Value cache from GPU HBM to CPU memory or NVMe to save VRAM.
The purchase price of an asset plus the costs of operation.
terminology.rationale: Standard financial/hardware term. Use standard translation.

terminology.context_meaning: Internet slang meaning to completely dominate or outperform someone/something else.
terminology.translation: 性能碾压 / 降维打击
terminology.rationale: Gen-Z/internet slang adapted for tech. Needs a strong, colloquial Chinese tech equivalent.

terminology.context_meaning: Internet slang for maximizing one's appearance, used here to mean aggressive corporate marketing/embellishment.
terminology.translation: 营销包装 / 疯狂画饼
terminology.rationale: Translating literally as '颜值最大化' makes no sense in a GPU context. It means making the product look better on paper than in reality.

terminology.context_meaning: Slang for acting foolishly or wasting time on unproductive tasks.
terminology.translation: 无脑跑测 / 徒劳折腾
terminology.rationale: Context is running benchmarks every single night wastefully. Needs a colloquial translation.

terminology.context_meaning: 如 7nm, 5nm, 3nm，需要注意翻译时不要语义重复，如 N3 node直接翻译成3纳米制程
基于人类反馈的强化学习
一种深度学习模型架构
只有表示大模型等的评测方式时翻译成基准测试
每秒万亿次运算 (Trillions Operations Per Second)
垂直整合制造商 (Integrated Device Manufacturer)
大模型的预填充阶段，常和decode一起出现
大模型的解码阶段，常和prefill一起出现
连续出现可以适当缩写成德州

style.tone: Highly technical, authoritative, yet casually dropping internet slang and cynical industry critiques.
style.target_audience: AI hardware engineers, ML inference optimization researchers, cloud infrastructure architects, and tech investors.
style.translation_voice: Professional and technically rigorous in engineering explanations, but retaining the sarcastic, direct, and slightly cynical 'tech-bro' flavor when critiquing corporate marketing or software failures.

challenges.location: Section: DeepSeek Disagg +WideEP Results Deep Dive
challenges.issue: Slang terms like 'framemog' and 'mogged' are used to describe performance benchmarks.
challenges.suggestion: Translate as '性能碾压' or '被按在地上摩擦' to capture the informal, aggressive tone typical of hardware enthusiast communities.

challenges.location: Section: Jensen Under Promising and Overdelivering
challenges.issue: References to 'Jensen Math' and 'reality distortion field'.
challenges.suggestion: Translate 'Jensen Math' as '老黄数学' or '老黄刀法' (widely understood in Chinese tech circles). Translate 'reality distortion field' as '现实扭曲力场' (Steve Jobs reference, well-known in China).

challenges.issue: Analogy of 'riding a metro bus vs a race car'.
challenges.suggestion: Translate naturally: '这就像是乘坐公交车与驾驶赛车的区别。公交车能同时服务许多乘客，但频繁停靠会消耗时间；而其成本可以分摊到众多乘客身上。'

challenges.location: Section: AMD Composability Issue on FP4
challenges.issue: 'Composability' in this context refers to software features working together, not hardware components.
challenges.suggestion: Ensure the translation uses '软件特性的可组合性' to avoid confusion with hardware modularity.

challenges.issue: Sarcastic tone about GitHub Copilot being free ('it became apparent why GitHub was giving it away for free').
challenges.suggestion: Maintain the sarcasm: '我们很快就发现 Copilot 简直糟透了，这也解释了为什么 GitHub 愿意白送这个功能。'

guidelines: Maintain technical precision for AI inference metrics (e.g., tok/s/user, TPOT, ISL/OSL). Do not translate acronyms like TP, EP, DP, but provide Chinese equivalents on their first appearance.
Capture the internet-native, cynical tone of the authors. Translate slang like 'framemog' as '性能碾压' and 'lookmaxxing' as '营销包装/疯狂画饼' to match the tech-community vibe.

Sentence Restructuring: For complex English sentences explaining parallelism (e.g., 'EP shards experts... with the drawback being an all-to-all collective...'), break them into shorter, logical Chinese clauses: 'EP 旨在切分专家权重以利用 MoE 的稀疏性。然而，其缺点在于 MoE 层需要执行 All-to-all 集合通信...'
Clearly distinguish between 'Prefill' (首代词/预填充) and 'Decode' (解码) when discussing Disaggregated Prefill, ensuring the memory vs. compute tradeoff is accurately conveyed.

When discussing 'composability' (可组合性) in the AMD section, ensure the translation clearly implies software feature interoperability (i.e., using FP4, WideEP, and Disagg simultaneously), not hardware modularity.
Preserve the 'Jensen Math' (老黄数学) and 'reality distortion field' (现实扭曲力场) cultural references, as they are established memes in the Chinese hardcore hardware community.
Ensure cross-domain terms like 'Unit Economics' are translated as '单位经济效益' and 'TCO' as '总拥有成本 (TCO)', maintaining a professional financial analyst tone in the economics sections.

00-intro.role_in_article: Metadata and keyword seeding, establishing the temporal and technical scope of the benchmark report.
00-intro.relation_to_previous: N/A - This is the opening metadata section.
00-intro.relation_to_next: Provides the raw vocabulary and context that the formal introduction will expand into a narrative.
key_points: The benchmark covers cutting-edge hardware from both NVIDIA (GB300 NVL72, B200, H100) and AMD (MI355X).
Focuses on advanced serving techniques like Disaggregated Serving and Wide Expert Parallelism.
Evaluates major frameworks including SGLang, vLLM, and TRTLLM.
translation_notes: Keep hardware SKUs and framework names in English.
Establish standardized Chinese translations for core concepts early: 'Disaggregated Serving' (分离式服务), 'Wide Expert Parallelism' (宽专家并行), 'Large Mixture of Experts' (大型混合专家模型).

01-introduction.role_in_article: Establishes the credibility, methodology, and open-source ethos of the InferenceXv2 project.
01-introduction.relation_to_previous: Transforms the metadata keywords into a coherent project background and mission statement.
01-introduction.relation_to_next: Builds trust and context before delivering the hard empirical data in the 'Key Observations' section.
key_points: InferenceXv2 replaces static, point-in-time benchmarks with continuous, dynamic testing across hundreds of chips.
The project is fully open-source (Apache 2.0) to match the rapid pace of AI software evolution.
Provides free tooling (dashboard/visualizer) for the ML community to verify data independently.
translation_notes: Translate 'point-in-time benchmarks' accurately to convey static limitations (e.g., 静态的/特定时间点的基准测试).
Maintain a community-driven, welcoming tone while preserving the technical authority of the methodology.

02-key-observations-and-results-t.role_in_article: Delivers the executive summary of the benchmark's most critical findings regarding the AMD vs. NVIDIA rivalry.
02-key-observations-and-results-t.relation_to_previous: Fulfills the introduction's promise by immediately providing high-value, actionable benchmarking insights.
02-key-observations-and-results-t.relation_to_next: Sets up the need to acknowledge hardware partners before diving into the technical explanations of *why* these results occurred.
key_points: AMD's FP8 MI355X using SGLang with disaggregated prefill and WideEP matches NVIDIA's B200 SGLang performance.
NVIDIA maintains absolute dominance when using the highly optimized Dynamo TRTLLM stack.
AMD suffers from a severe lack of software composability; enabling multiple SOTA inference optimizations simultaneously breaks competitiveness.
AMD's roadmap requires a pivot toward software composability for FP4 and distributed inferencing.
translation_notes: The term 'framemog' is hardcore internet/gaming slang meaning to completely dominate in performance/frame rates. Translate with a strong equivalent like '性能全面碾压' or '呈碾压之势'.
Ensure 'composability' is translated accurately in a software architecture context (软件可组合性).

03-acknowledgments-and-inferencex.role_in_article: Builds institutional authority by demonstrating deep industry access and provides a monetization/support CTA.
03-acknowledgments-and-inferencex.relation_to_previous: Balances the previous section's critique of AMD by highlighting exclusive, massive-scale access to NVIDIA's latest hardware.
03-acknowledgments-and-inferencex.relation_to_next: Acts as a structural bridge, closing the executive summary phase before transitioning into the educational technical primer.
key_points: The team had exclusive, independent testing access to nearly 1,000 NVIDIA GPUs, including the unreleased GB300 NVL72.
The benchmark pushes the Pareto frontier for GPT-OSS performance optimizations.
The project relies on reader support (free/paid subscriptions) to maintain its open-source nature.
translation_notes: Ensure NVIDIA executives' names are correctly transliterated (Jensen Huang -> 黄仁勋) or kept in English if preferred by the target publication style.
Translate 'Pushing Pareto Frontier' as '推进帕累托前沿', ensuring the economic/optimization context is clear.

04-a-primer-on-important-technica.role_in_article: Acts as a structural signpost, warning readers of a shift from high-level summaries to foundational technical theory.
04-a-primer-on-important-technica.relation_to_previous: Pivots the article from what the results are to the underlying physics of how LLM serving works.
04-a-primer-on-important-technica.relation_to_next: Directly introduces the specific tradeoff concepts detailed in the subsequent section.
key_points: Provides necessary technical context for interpreting complex charts and results.
Allows advanced readers to skip ahead while onboarding less technical readers.
translation_notes: Keep the translation brief and navigational. 'Primer' can be effectively translated as '入门指南' or '核心技术概念科普'.

05-interactivity-vs-throughput-tr.role_in_article: Defines the core economic and performance tension in LLM serving, establishing the metrics used throughout the benchmark.
05-interactivity-vs-throughput-tr.relation_to_previous: Executes the promise of the primer by defining the first and most important technical concept.
05-interactivity-vs-throughput-tr.relation_to_next: Establishes the baseline tradeoff (latency vs throughput) that the parallelization strategies in the next section attempt to optimize.
key_points: Interactivity (tok/s/user or TPOT) dictates user experience, while Throughput (tok/s) dictates system capacity.
Batching increases total throughput but reduces FLOPs allocated per request, slowing down individual generation.
Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) inversely correlates with throughput; higher throughput amortizes fixed hardware costs.
Disaggregated prefill separates compute-heavy prefill from memory-heavy decode to optimize these pools independently.
translation_notes: Strict terminology consistency required: 'Interactivity' (交互性/响应速度), 'Throughput' (吞吐量), 'TPOT' (每个输出token的时间).
Translate 'Disaggregated prefill' as '分离式Prefill' or '预填充与解码分离', and note the 'PD disaggregation' abbreviation.

06-tensor-parallel-expert-parall.role_in_article: Explains the distributed computing topologies used to manipulate the interactivity/throughput tradeoff.
06-tensor-parallel-expert-parall.relation_to_previous: Provides the engineering solutions (TP/EP/DP) to the fundamental tradeoffs outlined in the previous section.
06-tensor-parallel-expert-parall.relation_to_next: Equips the reader with the vocabulary needed to understand the specific configuration updates discussed in the timeline tracking section.
key_points: Tensor Parallelism (TP) maximizes interactivity for small batches but incurs heavy all-reduce communication overhead.
Expert Parallelism (EP) leverages MoE sparsity but requires expensive all-to-all collectives and suffers from small-batch imbalance.
Data Parallelism (DP) replicates models to load balance requests, scaling overall throughput.
translation_notes: Do not translate the acronyms (TP, EP, DP) without their English counterparts.
Use precise distributed systems terminology: 'all-reduce' (全归约), 'all-to-all collective' (全对全集合通信), 'shards experts' (专家分片), 'ranks' (节点/进程序列).

07-tracking-improvements-over-tim.role_in_article: Justifies the continuous, dynamic methodology of InferenceX by contrasting hardware and software development cycles.
07-tracking-improvements-over-tim.relation_to_previous: Connects the theoretical parallelization strategies back to the operational reality of the benchmark: software implementations change rapidly.
07-tracking-improvements-over-tim.relation_to_next: Sets up the specific chronological case study of DeepSeek R1's software optimizations on AMD.
key_points: Hardware iterations occur on an order-of-magnitude yearly cadence, whereas software optimizations occur weekly.
Benchmarking must involve constantly updating recipes to reflect the latest software state, not just static hardware specs.
translation_notes: The notation 'O(yearly)' and 'O(weekly)' uses Big O notation for order of magnitude. Translate to convey this specific meaning, e.g., '以年为数量级/周期' and '以周为数量级/周期'.

08-deepseek-r1.role_in_article: Provides a concrete, empirical case study demonstrating both the benchmark's value and AMD's rapid software progress.
08-deepseek-r1.relation_to_previous: Applies the 'tracking improvements over time' concept directly to a specific model (DeepSeek R1) and hardware vendor (AMD).
08-deepseek-r1.relation_to_next: Transitions into further specific model benchmarks (like GPT-OSS 120B) or detailed hardware comparisons.
key_points: AMD doubled throughput for SGLang DeepSeek R1 FP4 at the same interactivity level in under two months.
InferenceX successfully pressured AMD to upstream performance-enhancing forks into the official SGLang repository.
Recent SGLang releases have yielded measurable improvements for MI300X and MI325X architectures.
translation_notes: Translate 'upstream' as a verb accurately in the open-source context (e.g., '推送到上游' or '合并到官方主分支').
Maintain an objective but encouraging tone regarding AMD's software velocity, ensuring exact preservation of model (DeepSeek R1) and datatype (FP4) names.
